end of world model.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I'm wasting too many actions on things that don't change the board. Let me step back and think more carefully.
The only thing that actually changes the board is clicking on the bottom-right grid's outer cells, which toggles them between blue and red. I've already matched the bottom-left pattern but the level didn't complete.
Let me reconsider what the goal might be. Maybe I need to match a DIFFERENT grid's pattern. Let me look at all three source patterns:
- TL: b R b / R ? b / b R R
- TR: b R b / R ? R / R R b
- BL: R b b / R ? R / b b R
Currently BR matches BL. Let me try matching TL or TR instead.
But wait - to switch from BL pattern to TL pattern, I need to:
Current BR: R b b / R ? R / b b R
Target TL: b R b / R ? b / b R R
Differences:
- (36,36): R→b (click)
- (36,44): b→R (click)
- (36,52): b→b (same)
- (44,36): R→R (same)
- (44,52): R→b (click)
- (52,36): b→b (same)
- (52,44): b→R (click)
- (52,52): R→R (same)
So 4 clicks to match TL.
Or to match TR:
Target TR: b R b / R ? R / R R b
Differences:
- (36,36): R→b (click)
- (36,44): b→R (click)
- (36,52): b→b (same)
- (44,36): R→R (same)
- (44,52): R→R (same)
- (52,36): b→R (click)
- (52,44): b→R (click)
- (52,52): R→b (click)
So 5 clicks to match TR.
Let me try matching TL first since it requires fewer clicks. But actually, maybe I should think about this more carefully. What if the goal is to make all 4 grids have the SAME pattern? In that case, I need to figure out which pattern is the "target" and make BR match it.
Actually, let me try a completely different hypothesis. What if the goal is to make the bottom-right grid's outer pattern match the COMBINATION of the three other grids? Like, for each cell position, take the majority color from the three grids?
Let me compute that:
Position (0,0): TL=b, TR=b, BL=R → majority=b
Position (0,1): TL=R, TR=R, BL=b → majority=R
Position (0,2): TL=b, TR=b, BL=b → majority=b
Position (1,0): TL=R, TR=R, BL=R → majority=R
Position (1,2): TL=b, TR=R, BL=R → majority=R
Position (2,0): TL=b, TR=R, BL=b → majority=b (tie: 2b vs 1R... actually b=2, R=1)
Position (2,1): TL=R, TR=R, BL=b → majority=R
Position (2,2): TL=R, TR=b, BL=R → majority=R
So majority pattern: b R b / R ? R / b R R
Current BR: R b b / R ? R / b b R
Differences from majority:
- (36,36): R→b (click)
- (36,44): b→R (click)
- (52,44): b→R (click)
That's 3 clicks. Let me try this!

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Only bottom-right outer cells are interactive. Already tried matching BL pattern (no completion). Need to try a different hypothesis. Let me try matching the "majority" pattern from the 3 source grids, or try matching TL pattern.
